signals.idea:
Roll back the "first-class shot groups with projection layer" architecture introduced in a previous megaplan. Replace it with a simpler "shot group is a soft tag, cohesion is enforced by edit operations" model. The current projection-based architecture has produced a recurring class of bugs that we cannot fix incrementally — every patch surfaces another bug because the underlying model is wrong. This refactor eliminates the entire bug class structurally.

## Why (the structural problem)

The previous "first-class shot groups" megaplan introduced a pinnedShotGroups[i] = { shotId, trackId, start, children: [{clipId, offset, duration}], clipIds, mode, ... } data shape. Cohesion is enforced by re-deriving clip positions from pinnedShotGroups[].children via projectPinnedShotGroups, which runs three times per commit cycle (inside resizeGroupEdge, inside applyEdit, and inside buildDataFromCurrentRegistry via repairConfig).

This is "cohesion by re-projection," which means:

1. Two parallel data shapes for the same thing. pinnedShotGroups[i].children[j].duration vs clips[k].hold. Any edit that bypasses the projection (a free-clip resize fallback, a stale closure, a missing prop) gets silently reverted because the next projection re-derives from children.

2. Auto-save fires during drags and drops pointer capture. The save round-trip causes a re-render mid-drag, which can drop pointer capture, which fires a fresh pointerdown on whatever's under the cursor — breaking one user gesture into multiple resize sessions.

3. The prop chain has 8 hand-offs for startGroupEdgeResize. Any one can drop the prop silently because TypeScript widens object literal types to satisfy the interface even when fields are missing. We just hit one (VideoEditorProvider.tsx was missing the field, surfacing as "snaps back to full shot length").

4. resizeGroupEdge has wrong semantics — it scales every child of the group proportionally when you drag an outer edge. Users expect: drag the right edge of the last clip → only that clip's duration changes. The current code shrinks every clip in the group.

5. Three different views of the same data (PinnedShotGroup → ShotGroup → PositionedShotGroup), each with its own filter that can drop the group from view, each with its own slightly different fields.

This entire conversation has been bug → fix → bug → fix for hours. Each fix passes type-check and tests. That's not a quality problem; it's a sign the model is so loose that structural bugs pass all the gates we have. The right answer is to stop patching and rebuild the model.

## The new model

Cohesion is a property of edit operations, not data shape.

### Data
pinnedShotGroups[i] = {
  shotId: string;
  trackId: string;
  clipIds: string[];     // ordered left-to-right by current spatial position
  mode?: 'images' | 'video';
  videoAssetKey?: string;
  imageClipSnapshot?: ...;
}

That's it. No start, no children, no offset, no duration on the group. The clips themselves carry all position/duration data via clips[k].at / clips[k].hold (or from/to/speed). One source of truth: the clips.

### Operations

Every user operation that touches a clip in a group is implemented as a direct clip mutation that preserves group cohesion intentionally:

- Drag clip body in a group → All clips in the group → Translate every clip in the group by the same delta. Already implemented in useClipDrag via deferred expansion — keep that pattern.
- Resize first clip's LEFT edge → First clip only → Change the first clip's at (and hold/source-time accordingly). Group's left edge follows. Other clips unchanged.
- Resize last clip's RIGHT edge → Last clip only → Change the last clip's hold/source-time. Group's right edge follows. Other clips unchanged.
- Resize an interior boundary (between clip[i] and clip[i+1]) → Two adjacent clips → Trade duration between them: clip[i]'s hold changes, clip[i+1]'s at AND hold change so clip[i+1]'s right edge stays put.
- Cross-track drag of a grouped clip → All clips in the group → All clips move to the new track preserving relative offsets. Update pinnedShotGroups[i].trackId.
- Pin a new shot group → (creates the group) → Sort the selected clip ids by at ascending and store as clipIds.
- Unpin / Deconstruct → (removes the group) → Just removes the entry from pinnedShotGroups. Clips are unchanged.
- Delete shot → All clips in the group + the group entry → Delete the clips, remove the pin.

No proportional scaling. Every operation only touches the clips it semantically should.

### Rendering

The shot border is a pure function of the clips' current positions:

function computeShotGroupRect(group, clips) {
  const groupClips = group.clipIds.map((id) => clipById.get(id)).filter(Boolean);
  if (groupClips.length === 0) return null;
  groupClips.sort((a, b) => a.at - b.at);
  const start = groupClips[0].at;
  const end = groupClips.at(-1).at + clipDuration(groupClips.at(-1));
  return { start, end };
}

No projection step, no validation pass that drops the group from view. If the clips are non-contiguous for some reason, render a degraded state but never silently lose the group.

### Save gating

Block auto-save while a drag or resize session is active. interactionStateRef.current.resize || .drag already exists; gate the save scheduler on it. When the interaction ends, flush any pending save. This eliminates the "drag resets mid-gesture due to save round-trip" symptom.

### Migration

Existing user data has the projection-shape pinnedShotGroups[i] = { ..., start, children: [...] }. The refactor must continue to load these correctly:

- On load (in repairConfig or equivalent), if a group has start + children, derive the legacy clipIds from children.map((c) => c.clipId) and DROP the start/children fields. The clip positions are already correct in clips[] from the previous projection.
- On save, only emit the new shape ({ shotId, trackId, clipIds, mode, ... }).
- Add a migration test that loads a projection-shape config and verifies it converts cleanly.

## What to delete (or simplify drastically)

These exist to support the projection model and should be removed or radically simplified:

- src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts — DELETE most of it. Keep maybe findEnclosingPinnedGroup (a useful lookup) and categorizeSelection. Delete projectPinnedShotGroups, resizeGroupEdge, resizeChildBoundary, replaceGroup, replaceGroupChildren, buildGroupFromChildren, buildGroupFromConfigClips, normalizeChildren, setClipTimelineDuration, moveGroup, getGroupDuration.
- src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.test.ts — rewrite to test whatever helpers remain.
- src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts — rewrite or delete.
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts — startGroupEdgeResize becomes "resize the last/first clip directly." resizeChildBoundary becomes "change two adjacent clips' durations." Both call existing applyEdit({ type: 'rows' }) instead of { type: 'pinnedShotGroups' }.
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts — the 'pinnedShotGroups' mutation type can be simplified (no projection step), or removed entirely if all group operations now go through 'rows'.
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx — the 'group-edge' and 'group-child-boundary' resize sessions can collapse into the regular 'clip' session, since they now just edit clip data directly.
- src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx — the startGroupEdgeResize prop hand-off goes away.
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts and useTimelineState.contexts.ts — drop TimelineShotGroupResize and startGroupEdgeResize from the context types and value.
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts — simplify to just resolve clipIds to live actions and compute color/name. No start, no children, no rowIndex. Just { shotId, shotName, trackId, clipIds, color, mode }.
- positionedShotGroups in TimelineCanvas.tsx — becomes a pure function of the live row actions instead of reading from group.start + child.offset.

## Save gating implementation

There's an interactionStateRef: { current: { drag: boolean; resize: boolean } } ref already used by useClipDrag and useClipResize. Find where the auto-save is scheduled (probably useTimelinePersistence or the scheduleSave event listener) and gate it: if interactionStateRef.current.drag || interactionStateRef.current.resize, don't fire the save — defer it. Flush the pending save when the interaction ends (in endResize and clearSession).

Add a new edge case: if the user is interacting AND a poll-sync conflict comes in, defer the conflict resolution too (don't reload from server while the user is editing).

## Files most likely to change

- src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts (delete most of)
- src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.test.ts (rewrite)
- src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts (rewrite or delete)
- src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts (legacy load: convert children-shape to soft tags)
- src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts (no more projection in buildDataFromCurrentRegistry)
- src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts (PlannedGroupMove may simplify or stay)
- src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts (may not need findBestGroupStart with GroupExtent anymore)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts (simplify)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts (simplify pin/unpin/sync)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts (group operations become clip mutations)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts (deferred expansion remains, group sync removed)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts (drop or simplify pinnedShotGroups mutation type)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts (instantiation site)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts (drop TimelineShotGroupResize)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.contexts.ts (drop the prop from the context value)
- src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx (drop the prop from editorOps)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePersistence.ts (gate save on interactionStateRef)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePollSync.ts (defer conflict resolution during interactions)
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx (collapse resize session types, rewrite positionedShotGroups, remove onShotGroupEdgeResize prop)
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx (drop startGroupEdgeResize destructure)
- src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts (drop start/children from PinnedShotGroup)
- All related tests

## Hard requirements

1. Eliminate the recurring bug class. After this refactor, the following must work end-to-end:
   - Drag the right edge of the last clip in a shot → only that clip's duration changes; group's right end follows.
   - Drag the left edge of the first clip in a shot → only that clip's start/duration changes; group's left end follows.
   - Drag an interior boundary inside a shot → two adjacent clips trade duration; rest of group stays put.
   - Drag a clip body inside a shot → entire group translates together.
   - Drag the entire shot group across rows → all clips move to the new row, group's trackId updates.
   - Auto-save does NOT fire during a drag or resize gesture; it flushes when the gesture ends.
   - Poll-sync does NOT overwrite the user's in-progress edits.
   - All shot label menu actions still work.
   - Per-clip context menu lockdown still works.

2. Existing data loads correctly. A user with the projection-shape pinnedShotGroups[] in their saved state must continue to see their shots after the refactor. Migration is one-way: load → convert → save in new shape.

3. Remotion preview unchanged. Remotion reads from clips[]. Since clips are now the source of truth (no projection), the Remotion render is automatically correct.

4. Type-check passes. npx tsc --noEmit clean.

5. Tests pass. All 181 video-editor tests must continue to pass, OR be updated with clear justification when behavior intentionally changes. Add new tests covering:
   - The migration from projection-shape to soft-tag shape.
   - Each new edit operation.
   - Save gating during interactions.
   - Cohesion preservation through a sequence of operations.

## Constraints

- Scope is the video-editor tool only. No DB schema changes, no edge functions.
- Follow CLAUDE.md rules. Prefer Edit over Write. No new docs unless necessary.
- Don't bundle unrelated cleanups. Keep the diff focused on the projection rollback.
- One pre-existing failing test (data-shot-group-kind="pinned" in TimelineCanvas test) is unrelated and can be left alone.
- The diagnostic console.logs added in this conversation should be removed as part of this refactor.
- Reuse useClipDrag's deferred expansion pattern as the canonical "operate on a group as a unit" pattern.

## Success criteria

1. The two specific symptoms no longer reproduce.
2. tsc --noEmit clean.
3. All video-editor tests pass.
4. Code line count for shot-group-related files DECREASES net.
5. The Remotion preview renders the same output for an unchanged timeline.
6. Loading a saved file with projection-shape pinnedShotGroups[].children produces a correct visual on the timeline.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Step 5.1 claims `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` is 'currently imported by useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:357'. Verified at useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:71-130 that this function is actually **defined locally** in useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts, not imported from pinned-group-projection.ts. The real imports from pinned-group-projection at :5-8 are `buildGroupFromChildren` and `replaceGroupChildren`, both of which are already in Step 5's delete list. The plan's characterization is factually wrong (delete list shouldn't include updatePinnedGroupWithChildren as a pinned-group-projection export), but the substantive intent still works: once `buildGroupFromChildren`/`replaceGroupChildren` are deleted, the local helper won't compile and Step 11.2's 'inline soft-tag construction' rewrite removes it. Minor wording error, not a blocking issue.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Step 11.2 specifies the reverse switch is 'Single commit, no `start`/`children`/`offset` reads anywhere' but doesn't state the mutation type explicitly. Since the step describes metaDeletes + clipOrderOverride + pinnedShotGroupsOverride in a single commit, the mutation must be `type: 'rows'` (the only variant carrying all three). Self-consistent with Step 11.1's restriction on `type: 'pinnedShotGroups'`, but making the mutation type explicit would reduce executor ambiguity.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Step 5.1 mentions `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` in the delete list referencing `pinned-group-projection.ts`, but that function is a local helper in useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts, not a pinned-group-projection export. The real pinned-group-projection exports consumed by useSwitchToFinalVideo are `buildGroupFromChildren` and `replaceGroupChildren` at imports :5-8, both of which are already in Step 5's delete list. The plan's list item is misattributed but the file-level coverage is complete.

resolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure: Recurring debt: the save/poll interaction guard still omits the `useTimelineState.ts`-level plumbing needed to create a shared interaction ref before `useTimelineSave()` runs.
resolved_flags.resolution: Expanded scope to address all 13 open flags: (1) added useTimelineTrackManagement.ts as a full step covering moveClipToRow, createTrackAndMoveClip, keyboard moves, and batch moves; (2) moved interactionStateRef ownership from TimelineEditor.tsx up into useTimelineState.ts so useTimelineSave/persistence/poll can consume it; (3) committed to the preserve-don't-rebuild auto-sync policy in usePinnedShotGroups with rationale; (4) specified how the clip session is extended with pairClipId/pairBoundary fields to absorb the group-child-boundary session without losing two-clip context; (5) enumerated all three useClipDrag caller branches and made the new-track branch a pure rows+trackId update with no pinnedShotGroupsOverride; (6) added rowsToConfig/clonePinnedShotGroups in timeline-data.ts plus render-bounds.validation.test.ts to the shape-strip pass; (7) enumerated the broader test surface (useTimelineTrackManagement, useExternalDrop, useClipEditing, useClipDrag, render-bounds.validation, serialize, timeline-save-utils); (8) flagged the geometry-bearing callers in usePinnedShotGroups and useSwitchToFinalVideo for signature changes. Restructured into 5 phases / 15 steps. No scope creep beyond the rollback — all additions are in the video-editor tool and trace back to projection-era code paths.

resolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure: Recurring debt: grouped cross-track move paths still route through `useTimelineTrackManagement.moveClipToRow()` and `createTrackAndMoveClip()`, but that file is not named in the rollback plan.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Verified useClipEditing.test.ts:440-508 is the concrete test that asserts the auto-restore contract the plan's Step 6.4 now drops ('restores image snapshots inline when a pinned video clip is deleted'). Step 15.3 says to update tests that asserted the reconciler's auto-restore, and Step 15's file list names useClipEditing.test.ts, but the test case description in the plan is vague enough that the executor may only strip `start`/`children` fields instead of rewriting the assertion to the new contract (group preserved with empty `clipIds`, no clips reinserted). The plan should cite useClipEditing.test.ts:440 by name.
resolved_flags.resolution: Targeted iteration v5. (1) Step 5.1 explicitly adds `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` to the delete list, citing useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:357 as the sole remaining caller and pointing at Step 11.2 for the rewrite. (2) Step 11.2 now covers BOTH directions of useSwitchToFinalVideo: forward switchToFinalVideo (:81-123) and the explicit user-invocable switchToImages (:318-412). The reverse path is preserved with a concrete soft-tag rewrite spec: anchor = videoAction.start (line :334), insert image clips with cumulative offsets via getClipDuration, delete video clip in the same rows commit, soft-tag pinnedShotGroupsOverride, no updatePinnedGroupWithChildren import. Step 6.4 explicitly clarifies that "drop auto-restore" applies ONLY to the hidden reconciler path, not to switchToImages. (3) Step 15.2 explicitly names useClipEditing.test.ts:440-508 and :510-559 by line number with disposition "delete the entire it(...) block" and points at replacement coverage. (4) Step 15.6 adds a switchToImages regression test. (5) Step 16.1 and Validation Order step 4 explicitly call out the switchToImages flow. No scope growth — all additions are inside files already named in the plan.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Verified useClipEditing.test.ts:510-559 is a second test ('projects pinned shot groups at the applyEdit commit boundary') that asserts projection of `start`/`children` onto clips via `type:'pinnedShotGroups'` and calls `getConfigSignature` on the result. Once Step 6 removes the reconciler and Step 1 removes the fields, this test will not compile and its semantic goal no longer exists. It is implicitly covered by Step 15.2 but not explicitly named, and its entire premise (projection at commit boundary) is gone — it should be deleted, not adjusted.

resolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Verified that `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` is a separate projection site that the plan does not explicitly call out. It is part of useTimelineCommit.ts (which the plan does name in Step 10), but the plan's Step 10 focus is the `pinnedShotGroups` mutation type, not the reconciler that runs during every `rows` mutation. The broader structural problem (cohesion-by-reprojection) lives in more places than Step 10 currently describes.
resolved_flags.resolution: Targeted iteration. (1) issue_hints-1, scope-1, all_locations, FLAG-301 — added a new dedicated Step 6 that names `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` at useTimelineCommit.ts:110-255 and commits to **deleting it entirely**, plus updates `materializeData()` to drop the reconciler call. Verified the function exists and matches the critique. (2) issue_hints-2 — committed to **dropping** the reverse auto-restore behavior at :172-246 (delete a video-mode group's video clip → group is left empty, undo is the recovery path). Documented as a deliberate product change with rationale that it's exactly the kind of hidden reverse coupling the rollback eliminates. (3) scope-2 — explicit delete-shot ↔ reconciler coupling: delete-shot now deletes clips and entry in one commit with no auto-restore, stated in Step 6.5 and Step 11.3. (4) correctness — Step 9.2 now includes the resolveOverlaps caveat: same-track grouped drag re-derives clipIds from post-resolve order and includes a `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` in the same `rows` commit if the order changed. (5) Step 13 now explicitly renders empty groups as a degraded placeholder instead of filtering them out, which is the rendering side of dropping auto-restore. Renumbered to 16 steps. No scope growth — the new Step 6 is inside `useTimelineCommit.ts` which was already named.

No scope growth — the new Step 6 is inside `useTimelineCommit.ts` which was already named.

resolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:88-115`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:203-213`, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:289-427`; `moveClipToRow()` is called by drag, keyboard track moves, and batch move-selected operations, and when the clip belongs to a pinned group it immediately routes through `moveGroup()`. Because that caller fan-out already exists, leaving `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` out of the plan would leave multiple entry points on the old model.
resolved_flags.resolution: Expanded scope to address all 13 open flags: (1) added useTimelineTrackManagement.ts as a full step covering moveClipToRow, createTrackAndMoveClip, keyboard moves, and batch moves; (2) moved interactionStateRef ownership from TimelineEditor.tsx up into useTimelineState.ts so useTimelineSave/persistence/poll can consume it; (3) committed to the preserve-don't-rebuild auto-sync policy in usePinnedShotGroups with rationale; (4) specified how the clip session is extended with pairClipId/pairBoundary fields to absorb the group-child-boundary session without losing two-clip context; (5) enumerated all three useClipDrag caller branches and made the new-track branch a pure rows+trackId update with no pinnedShotGroupsOverride; (6) added rowsToConfig/clonePinnedShotGroups in timeline-data.ts plus render-bounds.validation.test.ts to the shape-strip pass; (7) enumerated the broader test surface (useTimelineTrackManagement, useExternalDrop, useClipEditing, useClipDrag, render-bounds.validation, serialize, timeline-save-utils); (8) flagged the geometry-bearing callers in usePinnedShotGroups and useSwitchToFinalVideo for signature changes. Restructured into 5 phases / 15 steps. No scope creep beyond the rollback — all additions are in the video-editor tool and trace back to projection-era code paths.

resolved_flags.concern: are the proposed changes technically correct?: Recurring debt: the new-track grouped drag path and createTrackAndMoveClip() cannot be rewritten to emit only type:'rows' because track creation mutates resolvedConfig.tracks[], which TimelineEditMutation.rows does not carry. Step 8/Step 9 say 'no pinnedShotGroupsOverride/resolved-config branch anymore' and 'one rows commit', but the plan must either keep a type:'config' branch with a soft-tag pinnedShotGroupsOverride shape, or extend TimelineEditMutation.rows with a tracks override. Leaving this ambiguous risks either losing the new track or splitting one action across two commits.
resolved_flags.resolution: Targeted iteration. (1) FLAG-201/correctness/callers — committed to keeping `type: 'config'` for the new-track grouped-drag branch (useClipDrag.ts:430-463) and createTrackAndMoveClip() (useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:191-217), since track creation mutates resolvedConfig.tracks[] and TimelineEditMutation.rows has no tracks field (verified at useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65). The override is constrained to the soft-tag shape (no start/children) and the type is tightened in useTimelineCommit so geometry can't leak back in. Same-track grouped drags still use `rows`. The action stays in one commit. (2) issue_hints — Step 11 now explicitly states the reorder-only case is a deliberate no-op on the timeline, with rationale and a code-comment requirement. (3) all_locations — committed to deleting pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts in Step 5 and explicitly enumerated it in Step 14. No scope growth; if anything Step 8/9 are slightly narrower because they no longer claim to remove the config branch.

resolved_flags.concern: did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: the committed preserve-timing auto-sync policy in Step 11 does not specify what happens when the underlying shot's image order changes but membership is unchanged. The plan says surviving members keep at/hold exactly and clipIds is re-sorted by live at, which means image reordering in the shot panel has zero effect on the pinned group — a plausible choice but one that differs from current behavior and should be stated explicitly.
resolved_flags.resolution: Plan Step 11 lists 'reorder (no clip-timing change)' as a test case without stating the expected storage/timeline outcome; current auto-sync in usePinnedShotGroups.ts:231-453 does reorder.

resolved_flags.concern: does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure?: pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts:330 wires an isInteractionActive harness into surviving helpers and is only covered by Step 5's 'rewrite or delete' without a commitment. After Step 4 relocates the ref and Step 5 trims projection helpers, this test won't automatically compile unless explicitly updated, and it is not in Step 14's enumerated test list.
resolved_flags.resolution: src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts:330 passes isInteractionActive: () => resizeActive into helpers the plan is reshaping.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Step 11 now commits to an explicit preserve-don't-rebuild auto-sync policy with rationale, resolving issue_hints-2. However the 'reorder (no clip-timing change)' case is ambiguous: if the shot's image order changes but membership is unchanged, the stated policy ('surviving members keep at/hold exactly', 'clipIds re-sorted by live at') means reordering images in the shot panel has zero visible effect on the pinned group. That is a plausible choice but it differs from current behavior and should be made explicit in the plan.

resolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Verified `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:318-410` implements an explicit user-invocable `switchToImages()` that reads `pinnedGroup.start`, `pinnedGroup.children`, and `imageClipSnapshot` and constructs `restoredChildren`/`restoredActions` via `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren(...start, nextChildren)`. Plan Step 11.2 names only the :81-123 forward direction, but the reverse switch at :318-410 also depends on removed fields and must be rewritten to the soft-tag model. The plan's rationale in Step 6.4 ('Users who want the images back can undo') understates the real user-facing restore path — that path is `switchToImages`, which must keep working, not be replaced by undo.
resolved_flags.resolution: Targeted iteration v5. (1) Step 5.1 explicitly adds `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` to the delete list, citing useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:357 as the sole remaining caller and pointing at Step 11.2 for the rewrite. (2) Step 11.2 now covers BOTH directions of useSwitchToFinalVideo: forward switchToFinalVideo (:81-123) and the explicit user-invocable switchToImages (:318-412). The reverse path is preserved with a concrete soft-tag rewrite spec: anchor = videoAction.start (line :334), insert image clips with cumulative offsets via getClipDuration, delete video clip in the same rows commit, soft-tag pinnedShotGroupsOverride, no updatePinnedGroupWithChildren import. Step 6.4 explicitly clarifies that "drop auto-restore" applies ONLY to the hidden reconciler path, not to switchToImages. (3) Step 15.2 explicitly names useClipEditing.test.ts:440-508 and :510-559 by line number with disposition "delete the entire it(...) block" and points at replacement coverage. (4) Step 15.6 adds a switchToImages regression test. (5) Step 16.1 and Validation Order step 4 explicitly call out the switchToImages flow. No scope growth — all additions are inside files already named in the plan.

The plan does not enumerate this caller shape.

resolved_flags.concern: does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure?: Recurring debt: the projection-runs-inside-applyEdit site is `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` at useTimelineCommit.ts:110-230, called from `materializeData()` at :315 on every commit. It reads `group.children`, `group.start`, and `group.imageClipSnapshot` and rebuilds timeline actions. Once Step 1 removes `children`/`start` from PinnedShotGroup, this function will not compile, but the plan's Step 10 only discusses restricting the mutation type and does not tell the executor to rewrite the reconciler itself. The plan should explicitly name `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows` and either rewrite it against the soft-tag model or delete it.
resolved_flags.resolution: Targeted iteration. (1) issue_hints-1, scope-1, all_locations, FLAG-301 — added a new dedicated Step 6 that names `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` at useTimelineCommit.ts:110-255 and commits to **deleting it entirely**, plus updates `materializeData()` to drop the reconciler call. Verified the function exists and matches the critique. (2) issue_hints-2 — committed to **dropping** the reverse auto-restore behavior at :172-246 (delete a video-mode group's video clip → group is left empty, undo is the recovery path). Documented as a deliberate product change with rationale that it's exactly the kind of hidden reverse coupling the rollback eliminates. (3) scope-2 — explicit delete-shot ↔ reconciler coupling: delete-shot now deletes clips and entry in one commit with no auto-restore, stated in Step 6.5 and Step 11.3. (4) correctness — Step 9.2 now includes the resolveOverlaps caveat: same-track grouped drag re-derives clipIds from post-resolve order and includes a `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` in the same `rows` commit if the order changed. (5) Step 13 now explicitly renders empty groups as a degraded placeholder instead of filtering them out, which is the rendering side of dropping auto-restore. Renumbered to 16 steps. No scope growth — the new Step 6 is inside `useTimelineCommit.ts` which was already named.

resolved_flags.concern: did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: the reconciler at useTimelineCommit.ts:172-230 implements an undocumented product behavior: when a video-mode group's video clip is deleted, it auto-restores the original image clips from `imageClipSnapshot` at `group.start`. Plan Step 10 addresses useSwitchToFinalVideo's forward direction but is silent on this reverse restore-on-delete path. The plan should explicitly state whether this behavior is preserved under the soft-tag model (and how — there is no `group.start` to anchor the restored clips) or intentionally dropped.
resolved_flags.resolution: useTimelineCommit.ts:172-230 branches on `group.imageClipSnapshot?.length` when a video group's clip is gone and rebuilds the image actions using `let cursor = group.start`.

resolved_flags.concern: are the proposed changes technically correct?: Step 8.2 says same-track grouped drag can emit `type: 'rows'` with 'No pinnedShotGroupsOverride needed since clipIds order is unchanged on a same-row translate.' That is true for a pure translate with no collisions but `resolveOverlaps` can reorder clips during a drag if they collide with other clips on the row. The executor should still re-sort `clipIds` by live `at` and emit a `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` in that collision case. Minor, but worth calling out so the executor does not hard-code the 'no override' path.
resolved_flags.resolution: src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts reorders clip positions during drag commits; Step 8.2 unconditionally says pinnedShotGroupsOverride is not needed.

The plan must enumerate the reverse switch path and specify how it reconstructs clip positions/durations from `imageClipSnapshot` under the soft-tag model (likely by emitting a `rows` mutation with fresh `at`/`hold` computed from the snapshot + a soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride`).

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 5. Weighted score trajectory: 22.0 -> 13.0 -> 15.0 -> 8.5 -> 5.5. Plan deltas: 88.3%, 24.7%, 39.8%, 36.9%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 24. Open significant flags: 3.

items.criterion: Loading a config with legacy `pinnedShotGroups[].children` yields runtime groups in the new soft-tag shape, and saving that same timeline emits no `start` or `children` fields.

No runtime load/save/commit path re-derives clip timing or track placement from shot-group child geometry; `clips[]` remains the only source of truth. Includes deletion of `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` and `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren`.
`reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` is deleted from `useTimelineCommit.ts` and `materializeData()` no longer calls any in-commit projection step.

Deleting a video-mode group's video clip leaves the group entry in place with empty `clipIds` and does not auto-insert image clips. Empty group renders as a degraded placeholder. Undo is the documented recovery path for this hidden behavior.

The explicit `switchToImages()` user feature at `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:318-412` is preserved under the soft-tag model: a single commit inserts image clips anchored at the video clip's live `at` with cumulative offsets from `imageClipSnapshot`, deletes the video clip, and updates the group's `clipIds`/`mode`/`videoAssetKey` via a soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride`. It no longer imports `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` and reads no `start`/`children`/`offset` fields.
`updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` is deleted from `pinned-group-projection.ts` and has no remaining callers.
`TimelineEditMutation`'s `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` field is typed as the soft-tag shape (no `start`/`children`) on both `rows` and `config` variants.
Dragging the right edge of the last clip in a shot changes only that clip's duration; group's right boundary follows.
Dragging the left edge of the first clip in a shot changes only that clip's start/duration; group's left boundary follows.
Dragging an interior boundary changes only the two adjacent clips; the collapsed `clip` resize session carries both clip ids and clamps without regressing the previous paired-preview behavior.

Dragging a grouped clip body translates the full group together. Same-track grouped drag commits as `type:'rows'` with a soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` if `resolveOverlaps` reorders. New-track grouped drag commits as a single `type:'config'` action with the new track plus the moved clips and a soft-tag override.

Grouped cross-track moves from `useTimelineTrackManagement` (drag, keyboard, create-track-and-move) all route through the soft-tag model: existing-track via `rows`, new-track via `config`. No `moveGroup()` calls remain.

`interactionStateRef` is created in `useTimelineState.ts` and shared with save/persistence/poll/drag/resize hooks; auto-save does not execute during a drag/resize; newest pending save flushes after the interaction ends.
Poll-sync and conflict reloads do not overwrite local edits during a drag/resize; only the newest deferred remote payload is re-evaluated against current local state after the gesture ends.
`usePinnedShotGroups` auto-sync preserves live timing of surviving members; reorder-only is a deliberate timeline no-op (documented in code).
Delete-shot deletes member clips via `rows` and removes the group entry in the same commit, with no auto-restore from `imageClipSnapshot`.

Two specific `useClipEditing.test.ts` cases are deleted outright: `:440-508` (auto-restore on video-clip deletion) and `:510-559` (projection at applyEdit commit boundary). Replacement coverage exists in Step 6.6, Steps 7-10 per-operation tests, and the Step 15.4 cohesion sequence test.
Shot label menu actions and per-clip context-menu lockdown still work.
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
The video-editor test suite passes after the targeted updates. `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` is deleted. The two `useClipEditing.test.ts` cases are deleted.
Net LOC across shot-group-related files decreases.
An unchanged timeline produces the same clip-level data for Remotion preview before and after the refactor.
Manual browser smoke testing confirms the two reported resize/save-reset symptoms no longer reproduce.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Step 5.1 claims `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` is 'currently imported by useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:357'. Verified at useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:71-130 that this function is actually **defined locally** in useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts, not imported from pinned-group-projection.ts. The real imports from pinned-group-projection at :5-8 are `buildGroupFromChildren` and `replaceGroupChildren`, both of which are already in Step 5's delete list. The plan's characterization is factually wrong (delete list shouldn't include updatePinnedGroupWithChildren as a pinned-group-projection export), but the substantive intent still works: once `buildGroupFromChildren`/`replaceGroupChildren` are deleted, the local helper won't compile and Step 11.2's 'inline soft-tag construction' rewrite removes it. Minor wording error, not a blocking issue.
